data_sources.VIXCLS: FRED VIXCLS (CBOE VIX official daily close)
data_sources.USD_CNY: China interbank FX market midpoint rate

caveats: BAA is Moody's seasoned Baa corporate bond yield, daily series from FRED DBAA
VXVCLS (VIX3M) FRED data has ~1-2 day lag; latest available is 2026-07-13
FFR target range last changed Dec 2025 (cut to 3.50-3.75%)
Market pricing ~80% probability of 25bp hike at Sep 2026 FOMC per CME FedWatch
DFII10 not directly fetched; real yield computed as DGS10 - T10YIE

layer1_csi.f29_zscore_component: 159 bp, well below 500 bp hard trigger
layer1_csi.correlation_breakdown: No signs of cross-asset correlation breakdown

layer2_macro_quadrant.justification: Rising real yields (2.33%, up ~53bp from June) + falling breakeven inflation (2.24%, down from ~2.40%)

layer3_mci.delta_rates: 10Y up 38bp in one month — tightening financial conditions
layer3_mci.delta_vix: VIX spike on Jul 17 (+12%) from Iran tensions, but sub-20

risk_flags: RISING REAL YIELDS — DGS10 up 38bp in 1 month to 4.57%, valuation headwind for growth/tech
VIX SPIKE — +12% on Jul 17 to 18.77 (Iran tensions, chip rout, Netflix guidance miss)
FOMC JUL 28-29 — Market pricing 80% chance of Sep hike; hawkish repricing risk
NASDAQ CORRECTION — -3.25% in 1 month, semiconductor selloff leadership
YIELD CURVE — Normal (+41bp) but steepening from tight spread; monitor inversion risk

allocation_tilt_recommendation.reasoning: Phase 1 Rising + Quadrant 4 Overheat → take lower end of equity range (65%), overweight real assets (20%) as overheat hedge, keep fixed income buffer (15%)

cross_validation.multi_source_method: FRED primary (macro) + Yahoo Finance (VIX live) + Morningstar/china interbank (FX)
cross_validation.date_reconciliation: All indicators cross-referenced to Jul 16-17, 2026 close
cross_validation.source_3_internal_consistency: VIXTERM = VIX - VIX3M consistent with WIF v5.9 formula
